climate.title: A single aspen grove in Utah called Pando is one organism sharing a 106-acre root system and 47,000 genetically identical trunks, weighs roughly 6,000 tons, and has been quietly cloning itself for somewhere between 9,000 and 80,000 years while every visible trunk above it lives and dies on a 130-year cycle.
climate.description:
Pando, a single quaking aspen in Utah's Fishlake National Forest, weighs 6,000 tons, contains 47,000 genetically identical trunks across 106 acres, and may have been cloning itself for up to 80,000 years.
